journal_entry.reason: Mondelez received $25,000 cash as a security deposit upon lease signing. Under IFRS, security deposits are recorded as liabilities because they represent an obligation to refund the tenant at the end of the lease term (subject to any deductions for damages or unpaid rent). This is not revenue - the monthly rent of $10,000 will be recognized as revenue each month as the lease term progresses. No sales tax applies to security deposits as they are not consideration for a taxable supply. The entry records the cash receipt (debit Cash) and the corresponding liability (credit Security Deposit Liability).

resolved_ambiguities.resolution: Security deposits are conventionally recorded as liabilities (refundable deposit payable) until conditions for retention are met or the deposit is returned
'Exclusive of sales tax' clearly indicates HST is not included in the $10,000 monthly rent and will be charged separately
The transaction describes receipt of security deposit only; no rent payment or period has elapsed, so no rent revenue is recognized at signing

output.asset_increase_reason: Cash increases by $25,000 from the security deposit received. This is a single asset increase despite the deposit representing a future obligation.
output.dividend_increase_reason: No owner withdrawals or dividends in this transaction.
output.expense_increase_reason: No expenses incurred. Receiving a security deposit does not create an expense.
output.liability_decrease_reason: No liabilities are being paid down or reduced. The security deposit creates a new liability (obligation to return the deposit), it does not decrease one.
output.equity_decrease_reason: No equity accounts are reduced. This is an operating transaction, not a distribution to owners or equity reduction.
output.revenue_decrease_reason: No revenue is being reversed or reduced. The security deposit is not revenue; it is a refundable deposit that creates a liability.

output.liability_increase_reason: The $25,000 security deposit received creates a liability (refundable deposit payable) because Mondelez has an obligation to return it to the tenant at the end of the lease term, subject to any deductions for damages.
output.equity_increase_reason: No equity accounts increase. The security deposit is not owner investment or retained earnings; it is a liability owed to the tenant.
output.revenue_increase_reason: No revenue is recognized at the time of receiving the security deposit. The deposit is refundable and not earned income. Rental revenue will be recognized monthly as the tenant occupies the space and time passes.
output.asset_decrease_reason: No assets decrease. Cash is received (debit to cash, an asset increase on the debit side), so there is no asset decrease on the credit side.
output.dividend_decrease_reason: No dividends or owner drawings are involved in this transaction.
output.expense_decrease_reason: No expenses decrease. This is a receipt of a security deposit, not a refund or reversal of an expense.

output.incomplete_information_reasoning: The transaction is clear. Mondelez received $25,000 cash as a security deposit upon lease signing. Under IFRS, a security deposit is a liability (refundable to the tenant at lease end, subject to any deductions for damages). The transaction explicitly states this is a security deposit received, not advance rent or revenue. No business facts are missing - the nature of the payment (security deposit) and amount ($25,000) are stated.

debit_asset_increase.reason: Accept. Cash received of $25,000 increases the asset account Cash.

debit_dividend_increase.reason: No dividend transaction in this lease security deposit receipt.

debit_expense_increase.reason: No expense incurred in receiving a security deposit.

debit_liability_decrease.reason: No liability is being reduced by receiving a security deposit.

debit_equity_decrease.reason: No equity reduction in this transaction.

debit_revenue_decrease.reason: No revenue reversal in this transaction.

credit_liability_increase.reason: Accept. Security deposit received creates a liability (obligation to refund to tenant at lease end). This is a refundable deposit, not revenue.

credit_equity_increase.reason: No equity contribution in this transaction.

credit_revenue_increase.reason: No revenue is recognized. Security deposits are liabilities, not revenue. Revenue will be recognized monthly as rent is earned.

credit_asset_decrease.reason: No asset is reduced by receiving cash.

credit_dividend_decrease.reason: No dividend reversal in this transaction.

credit_expense_decrease.reason: No expense reversal in this transaction.

entry.reason: Mondelez received $25,000 cash as a security deposit upon lease signing. Under IFRS, security deposits are recorded as liabilities because they represent an obligation to refund the tenant at the end of the lease term (subject to any deductions for damages or unpaid rent). This is not revenue - the monthly rent of $10,000 will be recognized as revenue each month as the lease term progresses. No sales tax applies to security deposits as they are not consideration for a taxable supply. The entry records the cash receipt (debit Cash) and the corresponding liability (credit Security Deposit Liability).

output.decision_rationale: The transaction is complete and unambiguous. A security deposit received is clearly a liability under IFRS (IAS 17/IFRS 16), representing an obligation to refund. The classifier tuples correctly identified this as debit asset increase (cash) and credit liability increase (deposit liability). The amounts are stated explicitly ($25,000). No tax applies to security deposits. The entry balances (debits $25,000 = credits $25,000) and follows proper accounting treatment. The monthly rent revenue recognition is a separate future transaction not recorded at lease signing.
